hook_line: Fear tastes like stone in the mouth.
full_narration:
[BEGINNING]
Fear tastes like stone in the mouth. [PAUSE] In darkness, Cronus swallows his newborns—each gulp a desperate, silent bargain with fate. [PAUSE] The world shrinks, love curdles; dread passes from father to child, unspoken yet heavy as granite.
[TURNING POINT]
Yet one child escapes. Rhea’s secret—the child hidden in shadows, raised apart. [PAUSE] Cronus grows hollow, haunted by what he cannot consume. [PAUSE] When Zeus returns, the sky splits. The gods rage, not for glory, but because they were broken first. [EMPHASIS]
[RESOLUTION]
Victory comes—Zeus crowned in the ruins of his father’s hunger. [PAUSE] But the taste of stone lingers. The throne is cold, the old wounds unhealed. [PAUSE] Power changes hands, but fear survives, waiting for its next heir.

audio_cues.description: Low subterranean rumble, faint scraping like stone against stone
Sparse, sustained single note from bowed string instrument
Cluster of dissonant strings and distant, metallic percussion rising
Echo of distant shattering stone, quick decay
Music fades to a brittle, trembling drone before silence

music_brief: Genre: Neo-classical minimalism with dark ambient textures. BPM: 40–60. Instrumentation: Prepared piano, solo cello, low taiko drum, sparse metallic percussion, subtle bowed glass. Emotional arc: Opening is sparse and cold, dominated by sustained cello and low drones; at the turning point, introduce dissonant layers and muted percussive hits as tension peaks; for the resolution, return to near silence with trembling drone, never resolving to warmth. Reference: Hildur Guðnadóttir's 'Chernobyl' score. Music must never become rhythmic, heroic, or lush—no swelling major chords or melodic motifs.
narration_style: Measured, never rushed; each word lands heavy, slight elongation on stressed syllables. [BEGINNING]: Voice carries a low, resonant tension with deliberate pacing—aged 40–60, rough-edged, accent neutral, minimal warmth, as if confiding a secret. [TURNING POINT]: Slight tightening of pace, increased sharpness on consonants, hint of breathlessness—no uplift in pitch. [RESOLUTION]: Slowest section, hollow and distant, trailing at the end of sentences. [PAUSE] markers: full stop, minimum 1-second silence to let the weight settle. [EMPHASIS]: Slight volume increase, lower pitch, words almost bitten off. Voice should never sound reassuring—maintain the sense of suffocating dread throughout.
